    "R1.1": "The section carries an explicit gap statement ('no prior work has used RL directly to optimize for calibration') and a contribution claim positioning CalibRL; reconstructing and stress-testing that framing is central to any substantive review.",
    "R1.2": "Multiple inferential steps are present: Brier vs. log-loss reward comparison, the causal attribution of bounded gradients to stability under PPO, and the generalization argument from TriviaQA to OOD splits — each requires logical consistency checking.",
    "R1.3": "Specific numerical claims (ECE 0.18→0.06 on TriviaQA, 0.21→0.09 on MMLU, accuracy within 0.4%, 1.7% drop for log-loss, ECE <0.10 on all OOD splits) must be audited for alignment with what the experiments actually establish.",
    "R1.4": "The 'to our knowledge' novelty claim is load-bearing for the paper's positioning; prior work on RL-for-calibration (even partial) must be checked against the cited literature and likely gaps.",
    "R1.7": "Five distinct citation clusters are used to support factual claims about prior work behaviour (overconfidence under RLHF, supervised verbalization); accuracy of attribution and coverage of the calibration literature are materially relevant."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Is the intended review a scientific content review — checking logical validity, novelty claims, and evidence-to-claim alignment — or a writing/editorial review focused on prose clarity, style, and section structure?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The section carries an explicit gap statement ('no prior work has used RL directly to optimize for calibration') and a contribution claim positioning CalibRL; reconstructing and stress-testing that framing is central to any substantive review.
rationale.R1.2: Multiple inferential steps are present: Brier vs. log-loss reward comparison, the causal attribution of bounded gradients to stability under PPO, and the generalization argument from TriviaQA to OOD splits — each requires logical consistency checking.
rationale.R1.3: Specific numerical claims (ECE 0.18→0.06 on TriviaQA, 0.21→0.09 on MMLU, accuracy within 0.4%, 1.7% drop for log-loss, ECE <0.10 on all OOD splits) must be audited for alignment with what the experiments actually establish.
rationale.R1.4: The 'to our knowledge' novelty claim is load-bearing for the paper's positioning; prior work on RL-for-calibration (even partial) must be checked against the cited literature and likely gaps.
rationale.R1.7: Five distinct citation clusters are used to support factual claims about prior work behaviour (overconfidence under RLHF, supervised verbalization); accuracy of attribution and coverage of the calibration literature are materially relevant.

parsed.clarification_question: Is the intended review a scientific content review — checking logical validity, novelty claims, and evidence-to-claim alignment — or a writing/editorial review focused on prose clarity, style, and section structure?
